data.title: Tantech receives Nasdaq non-compliance notice
data.description:
• Tantech Holdings (TANH) received a Nasdaq notice for failing the $1 minimum bid price requirement over 30 consecutive days.
  • The company has 180 days, until September 22, 2026, to regain compliance.
  • Compliance can be achieved if shares trade at or above $1 for at least 10 consecutive business days.
  • Tantech may use measures like a reverse stock split to meet the requirement.
  • If unmet, the company could qualify for an additional 180-day grace period or face potential delisting.

data.title: Mountain Valley plans C$2M private placement, debt settlement transactions
data.description:
• Mountain Valley (CSE: MVMD) (OTC: MVMDF) on Monday said that it intends to raise up to CAD $2 million through a non-brokered private placement of units priced at CDN $0.025 each.
  • Each unit will include one common share and one warrant exercisable at CDN $0.08 per share for 12 months, with potential acceleration if certain price conditions are met.
  • The offering is expected to close in April 2026, subject to regulatory approvals.
  • Separately, the company plans to settle CDN $485,000 in debt through two shares-for-debt transactions.
  • One tranche will cover CAD $385,000 at CDN $0.017 per share, while the second will settle CAD $100,000 at CDN $0.06 per share.
  • The company said the transactions are aimed at substantially eliminating outstanding debt and strengthening its balance sheet.
  • MVMDF closed +16.59% at $0.0548.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Fosun International reports FY results
data.description:
• Fosun International press release (FOSUF): FY Group's total revenue reached RMB173.43B.
  • Adjusted industrial operation profit amounted to RMB 4B.
  • The four core subsidiaries generated RMB128.2B in revenue, accounting for 74% of the Group's total revenue.

data.title: Conagra Brands declares $0.35 dividend
data.description:
• Conagra Brands (CAG) declares $0.35/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 8.9%
  • Payable June 3; for shareholders of record April 30; ex-div April 30.
  • See CAG Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: J. Jill sets disappointing guidance as tariff headwinds persist
data.description:
Shares of J. Jill (JILL) are pinned down in early trading Tuesday as better-than-feared fourth-quarter results and an increase in the dividend were overshadowed by disappointing guidance for both the first quarter and full year as the company continues to face tariff-related headwinds.

Although the company experienced another quarter of declining comparable and net sales and slipped into the red, the results were not as bad as anticipated, as a loss of $0.02 per share was better than expectations for a loss of as much as $0.12 per share. Net loss in Q4 included $3.1M in expenses related to the loss on debt refinancing.

Total sales were down 3.1% to $138.4M but $3M above estimates. This reflected a 4.8% drop in comparable store sales. Direct-to-consumer sales, which represent 53.5% of net sales, were up 2.6%.

Moreover, $4.5M in incremental tariff costs—net of vendor mitigation—compressed the company’s gross profit margin by 320 basis points to 63.1%.

For the current quarter, J. Jill (JILL) expects net sales to decline by 5% to 7%, translating to a range of $142.87M and $145.9M, which falls short of the consensus estimate of $156.2M.  Comparable sales are expected to be down 7% to 9% year-over-year, while gross margin is seen contracting another 400 basis points due to ~$5M of costs associated with tariffs. 

For the full year, net sales are expected to be flat to down 2%, suggesting a range between $584.6M and $596.5M, also less than the $610.8M estimates. Comparable sales are seen down by 1% to 3%, along with a 50-basis point margin compression to incorporate $15M of tariff costs.

At the end of the fourth quarter, the company declared an increased quarterly dividend of $0.09 per share—up a penny from the prior dividend—to be paid on April 28 to shareholders of record as of April 14. This equates to an annualized dividend of $0.36 per share.

Shares are expected to open with a new 52-week low.

data.title: China Gold International Resources declares $0.35 dividend

data.title: Banks reportedly charging more for loans to private credit funds
data.description:
U.S. banks are charging more for certain loans to private credit funds, implying a tightening of lending to the sector, Reuters reported, citing three people familiar ​with the matter.

The higher charges come amid concerns over the valuation given to some private credit investments, the sources reportedly said.

Earlier this month, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) ⁠reportedly marked ​down ‌the value ‌of ⁠certain loans ⁠held by private-credit groups ​and ​was tightening its ⁠lending to ⁠the ⁠sector.

Now people have questions about valuations that they didn't necessarily have six months ago. The questions are stressing the banks in terms of how much they're willing to lend, Seth Kleinman, chair of the special situations practice at law firm Benesch, told the newswire.

Private credit firms are also charging more, allowing ⁠them to absorb higher funding costs, according to one of the sources.

The broader concerns about private credit reportedly follow the collapse of Market Financial Solutions and First Brands, which impacted lenders including Barclays (BCS) (BCLYF); Banco Santander (SAN) (BCDRF); Wells Fargo (WFC); Apollo Global Management (APO) unit Atlas SP Partners; Jefferies Financial Group (JEF); Castlelake LP, majority owned by Brookfield (BN); and Western Alliance (WAL).

Furthermore, Blue Owl Capital (OWL) considering a merger of two of its private credit funds in a way that ​could impose losses ⁠on shareholders fueled the jitters, according to the report.

Separately, private credit lenders are said to be working with specific borrowers to avoid loan defaults.

The private credit lenders are looking to delay cash payments and extend concessions on their ​debt, Reuters said in a separate report.

Business development corporations extended private credit to smaller enterprises, and the software businesses that form the bulk of their clientele are struggling, noted Reuters on Tuesday.

BDCs are modifying loan agreements to include "payment in kind" or ‌PIK provision. The facility allows borrowers to defer cash interest payments, and instead add the dues to their loan principal, according to the newswire.

data.title: UK Royal Navy awards Teledyne contract for autonomous underwater vehicles and floats
data.description:
• Teledyne Marine, part of Teledyne Technologies (TDY) on Tuesday said it has been awarded a contract by the UK Ministry of Defence (MOD) in support of the Royal Navy’s Future Maritime Data Gathering (FMDG) - Persistent Oceanographic Data Collect.
  • Under this contract, Teledyne will supply numerous autonomous ocean observing systems, including Sentinel and Slocum gliders, APEX floats, and associated services, enabling the Royal Navy to expand its fleet of advanced unmanned technologies to collect high-quality oceanographic data in support of operational planning, maritime safety, and Defence activities, directly supporting Atlantic Bastion.
  • TDY +0.5% premarket to $587.02.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Crown Reserve SPAC to merge with Carvix in $1B deal
data.description:
• Crown Reserve Acquisition (CRAQ) will merge with Carvix in an all-stock transaction valuing Carvix at $1.0B.
  • The SPAC will redomicile to Delaware before closing, with the combined company remaining Nasdaq-listed.
  • Crown Reserve aims to raise at least $80M via PIPE and secure a $20M equity line, with $10M minimum cash required at closing.
  • Carvix shareholders may receive up to 50M earnout shares over four years tied to revenue and EBITDA targets.
  • The post-merger board will have five members, with Carvix nominating the majority and management staying in place.

data.title: Novo to sell Wegovy subscriptions on Hims, LifeMD, and others
data.description:
Novo Nordisk (NVO) on Tuesday announced a first-of-its-kind subscription program for its weight loss therapy Wegovy (semaglutide) available on telehealth platforms, including LifeMD (LFMD) and Hims & Hers (HIMS).

As part of the program, cash-paying patients can choose 3-, 6-, or 12-month subscriptions of Wegovy injection and Wegovy pill, starting on Mar. 31. Longer subscription periods will generate lower monthly pricing, the Danish drugmaker said.

"The new Wegovy® subscription program removes barriers, providing people who enroll a simple, affordable, and consistent way to start and stay on genuine, FDA-approved treatment," added Ed Cinca, senior vice president of Novo’s (NVO) Marketing & Patient Solutions.

According to the company, patients on a 12-month subscription plan can annually save up to $600 on the Wegovy pill and up to $1,200 on the Wegovy injection.

The program will be initially available through Ro, WeightWatchers (WW), and LifeMD (LFMD), with Hims & Hers (HIMS), Sesame, and others expected to join soon.
